title: Tulare, CA Reviews - Niche
content: Tulare is a nice and small town for people to settle down and who don't like to live around too much action. The view of the mountains are beautiful.

title: The biggest park - Review of Golden Gate Park, San Francisco, CA - Tripadvisor
content: # The biggest park - Golden Gate Park. *   San Francisco - Things to Do. **#20** of 1,166 things to do in San Francisco. *   Image 4 1 ### SF: Hop-on Hop-off Sightseeing Tour with Optional Sunset Tour 4.1 (3,108) from $60. # The biggest park. Review of Golden Gate Park. This is the biggest park which I've visited in my life. this is all only inside the golden gate park !!!! *   Image 13 10 ### The Best of San Francisco eBike Tour 5.0 (16) from $104. The best Segway tour in San francisco. My father and I just did a 1.5 hour guided Segway tour of golden gate park. You follow the leader,on the Segway and they take you off-roading to see all the monuments and statues and flowers and shrubbery in the park. All in all, a fun way to spend time in golden gate park. It was a fun experience, and nice to see the park while not on foot.

title: Review of Mission Dolores Park | San Francisco, California, North America - AFAR
content: # Mission Dolores Park. Mission Dolores Park, aka “Dolo”, is one of the most popular parks in San Francisco, especially when the sun is out, since this neighborhood is known for being one of the sunniest in the city. On a sunny weekend, you’ll be hard pressed to find a patch of green to plant yourself on, but that’s what makes Dolores Park so much fun. ### Experience the Old and the New at Mission Dolores in San Francisco. Looking for the old and the new San Francisco all in one neighborhood? Head to the Mission and visit the Mission Dolores, the oldest intact original mission in California, and the oldest building in San Francisco—it was founded in 1776, and has witnessed the city’s history all the way from the American Indians to the Gold Rush to the 1906 earthquake. After you’re done checking out the Mission, walk to Dolores Park to experience San Francisco today: People of all shapes, sizes, ages, races, sexualities, and genders lie on one lawn, relaxing and overlooking the city.
